# MACRO REPORT — 2026-04-29
**Delivered:** 12:07 UTC | **Generated by:** Claude claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

Global markets are navigating a high-stakes confluence of events: a "super central bank week" featuring rate decisions from the Fed, BoC, ECB, BoE, and BoJ, set against the backdrop of an ongoing U.S.-Iran war that has disrupted the Strait of Hormuz for over eight weeks and elevated energy prices to multi-year highs. The single most important variable for today's session is the Federal Reserve's rate decision and Jerome Powell's final press conference as Chair, with markets universally pricing a hold at 3.50%–3.75% but scrutinising the Fed's language on war-driven stagflation risks [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/fed-likely-hold-rates-steady-powell-prepares-possible-swan-song-2026-04-27/]. Equities are pulling back modestly from record highs as Mag 7 earnings season peaks, while oil markets remain volatile around U.S.-Iran diplomatic signals. A surprise breakthroug in Hormuz negotiations or a hawkish Fed pivot would be today's most disruptive tail risks [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/27/us-iran-peace-talks-stall-global-markets-stocks-oil-treasurys.html].

---

## 1. GLOBAL MACRO OVERVIEW

The macro backdrop is firmly defined by stagflationary pressures emanating from the Middle East conflict. U.S. CPI rose 3.32% year-over-year as of March 2026, with consumer prices accelerating to 3.3% in March from 2.4% in February — the biggest yearly jump since May 2024 — as Iran-war energy shocks transmitted rapidly into headline inflation [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/CPIAUCSL]. PPI has also surged with a 1.78% month-on-month reading, confirming pipeline inflation pressures remain acute [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/PPIACO]. The Fed funds effective rate stands at 3.64%, within the 3.50%–3.75% target band, with no cut expected at Wednesday's meeting and Moody's chief economist Mark Zandi stating he does not expect any cuts this entire year [Source: https://www.cbsnews.com/news/fed-rate-decision-april-2026-powell-final-meeting/].

The 10-year Treasury yield holds at 4.35% and the 2-year at 3.78%, generating a positive 57bp term premium that reflects inflation risk and fiscal concerns rather than growth optimism [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10]. CNBC data shows the 10-year trading around 4.36% intraday on Wednesday, with the 30-year at 4.949% and 2-year at 3.848% — all roughly flat as investors await the Fed decision [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/29/treasury-yields-fed-decision-powell-warsh-mpc.html]. The yield curve configuration confirms markets are pricing persistent elevated rates rather than an imminent easing cycle.

Equity markets are experiencing modest post-record consolidation. The S&P 500 closed at 7,138.80 (-0.49%), the Nasdaq 100 at 27,029.01 (-1.01%), and the Dow at 49,141.93 (-0.05%) on April 28 [Source: Yahoo Finance (^GSPC)]. This follows a period of surprising resilience, with global indices having recouped losses from the initial outbreak of the Iran war and hovering near record highs despite the ongoing energy shock [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/27/us-iran-peace-talks-stall-global-markets-stocks-oil-treasurys.html]. The VIX ticks modestly higher to 17.94 (+0.62%), suggesting contained but building anxiety ahead of the FOMC decision and major tech earnings [Source: Yahoo Finance (^VIX)].

The UAE's announced exit from OPEC represents a significant structural shift in energy markets, contributing to Brent's sharp -3.29% decline on Wednesday to $107.60 even as Hormuz disruptions kept supply tight, creating the unusual divergence between Brent (-3.29%) and WTI (+3.37%) [Source: https://apnews.com/article/stock-markets-opec-trump-oil-iran-rates-16286a529f0fbb34ed213005ffda74b2]. Goldman Sachs has raised its Q4 Brent forecast to $90/bbl and WTI to $83/bbl on tighter Middle East supply [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/goldman-sachs-raises-oil-price-forecasts-tight-supply-2026-04-26/]. Gold at $4,573.00 continues to trade well above the Reuters poll median forecast of $4,916 for 2026, supported by central bank demand, Fed independence concerns, and stagflation hedging [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-27/gold-rally-tipped-resume-despite-setback-over-iran-conflict-reuters].

---

## 2. CONFLICT / GEOPOLITICAL STATUS

- The U.S.-Iran war has kept the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed for more than eight weeks, disrupting roughly one-fifth of global oil and gas shipments and sustaining a material energy risk premium across all markets. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/28/oil-prices-us-iran-hormuz-negotiations-wti-brent-crude.html]
- Iran has offered a new proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, conditional on the U.S. lifting its naval blockade and ending hostilities; U.S. President Trump convened a meeting to discuss the proposal but maintained red lines including preventing Tehran from obtaining a nuclear weapon. [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/live/stock-market-today-tuesday-april-28-225503310.html]
- Trump scrapped plans over the weekend to dispatch envoys for Iran discussions, stalling the diplomatic track even as the Hormuz offer remains on the table. [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/live/stock-market-today-tuesday-april-28-225503310.html]
- Analysts at Lipow Oil Associates estimate that an immediate end to hostilities would reduce crude oil prices by $10/barrel, but a return to normal market conditions would take months. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/28/oil-prices-us-iran-hormuz-negotiations-wti-brent-crude.html]
- The UAE has announced it will exit OPEC, delivering a significant blow to the oil cartel's cohesion and contributing to near-term price volatility in energy markets. [Source: https://apnews.com/article/stock-markets-opec-trump-oil-iran-rates-16286a529f0fbb34ed213005ffda74b2]
- Ray Dalio has publicly warned that the U.S. economy is entering a "stagflationary period," amplifying concerns about the dual inflation-growth shock from the conflict. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/28/cnbc-daily-open-investors-look-past-warning-signs-to-send-stock-markets-soaring.html]
- The Bank of Japan voted in a split decision to hold rates steady, with a hawkish minority reflecting growing inflation fears tied to the war. [Source: https://www.globalbankingandfinance.com/yen-steady-boj-kicks-off-big-week-central-banks/]

### Disputed

- [Report states gold is "trading well above the Reuters poll median forecast of $4,916 for 2026"]: This is incorrect. Gold at $4,573 is **below** the $4,916 median forecast, not above it. The Reuters poll represents an upside target, not a level gold has exceeded — confirmed via https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-27/gold-rally-tipped-resume-despite-setback-over-iran-conflict-reuters

- [Report describes the 57bp term premium (10Y at 4.35% minus 2Y at 3.78%) as a "positive term premium"]: While arithmetically the 10Y–2Y spread is +57bp, the CNBC data shows the 2Y intraday at 3.848%, which would narrow this spread to approximately 51bp. More importantly, the report conflates the yield curve slope with "term premium" — these are distinct concepts. The bundle contains no term premium model data to confirm this characterization.

- [Brent topped $111 cited in the OilPrice.com article, and the report references "earlier sessions topping $111/bbl"]: The OilPrice.com article (published Apr 28) states Brent topped $111, but the CNBC Daily Open article from the same date reports Brent closing at $108.23. The $111 figure appears to be an intraday high, not a closing level — the report's phrasing is ambiguous but not clearly wrong. Flagged for precision.

- [Report states "U.S. nat gas remains relatively insulated from Iran war disruption compared to European TTF" and separately reports TTF as "N/A"]: The bundle contains no TTF price data whatsoever, so the comparative claim about U.S. nat gas being insulated relative to TTF cannot be verified from the bundle. The ICIS article references "rising LNG prices as Europe competes with Asia" but provides no TTF spot level.

### Additional Insights

1. **Brent/WTI spread compression is a high-conviction trade signal.** Brent at $107.60 (-3.29%) and WTI at $103.30 (+3.37%) on the same day is an extraordinary divergence — a ~$4.30 spread vs. the typical $3-5 Brent premium. The UAE OPEC exit primarily affects global/seaborne crude benchmarks (Brent), while U.S. domestic supply dynamics drive WTI independently. A reversion of this spread is highly likely; traders should monitor the Brent-WTI differential for mean-reversion opportunity. Confirmed via Yahoo Finance (BZ=F, CL=F).

2. **Goldman's Q4 Brent target of $90/bbl implies ~16% downside from current $107.60.** The report presents Goldman's raised forecast as bullish context, but the $90 Q4 target is actually well **below** current spot prices — implying Goldman expects significant price normalization in H2 2026, presumably contingent
